issues.title: PresenceBar avatar buttons lack keyboard focus styles and role attributes
issues.description: Avatar circles in PresenceBar are rendered as divs inside Tooltip triggers but lack explicit role='button', tabIndex=0, and visible focus ring styles. Screen readers cannot discover or activate them, and keyboard users see no focus indicator.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, find every avatar div that is wrapped by a Tooltip.Trigger (the colored circle with initials). BEFORE: These are plain divs with no tabIndex, no role, and no focus outline style. AFTER: Add tabIndex={0}, role='img' with aria-label={`${user.displayName} is ${statusLabel}`} to each avatar container div. Add a focus-visible outline style: outline: '2px solid ' + colors.accentOrange, outlineOffset: '2px' applied via onFocus/onBlur state or CSS :focus-visible. Also ensure the Tooltip.Trigger wrapping element uses asChild and renders a button element with min 44x44px touch target. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: EditConflictGuard banner not responsive on narrow screens
issues.description: The conflict warning banner uses a horizontal flex layout with fixed padding that causes text truncation and overlapping buttons at viewport widths below 480px. No media query or responsive wrap is applied.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, find the conflict banner JSX (the div containing the AlertTriangle icon, conflict message text, and action buttons like Refresh/Override). BEFORE: The banner uses display:'flex', flexDirection:'row' with no wrapping, causing overflow on mobile. Buttons and text overlap at widths < 480px. AFTER: Change to flexWrap:'wrap' on the banner container. Set the text span to flex:'1 1 auto', minWidth:200. Set the button group to flex:'0 0 auto', marginTop on mobile via a conditional: if the container is wrapping, buttons should appear below the text. Add gap: spacing.sm between items. Ensure touch targets on Refresh and Override buttons are min 44x44px with padding: '10px 16px'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: EntityPresence PresenceDots shows invisible placeholder when store not initialized
issues.description: When presenceStore.isInitialized is false, PresenceDots renders a 24px invisible div. This creates a layout gap with no explanation. It should render nothing (null) when not initialized, and show a subtle loading skeleton when initialized but data is pending.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx, in the PresenceDots component, find the early return when !isInitialized or viewers.length === 0. BEFORE: Both conditions return a hidden 24px div with visibility:'hidden', causing unexplained blank space. AFTER: When !isInitialized, return null (render nothing, no layout impact). When isInitialized but viewers.length === 0, also return null. Remove the hidden placeholder div entirely. This eliminates phantom spacing in list rows when presence is not active. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: PresenceBar does not handle Liveblocks connection errors gracefully
issues.description: useOthers from Liveblocks is called but there is no error boundary or fallback when the Liveblocks room connection fails (e.g., invalid API key, network outage). A thrown error crashes the entire page.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, wrap the main PresenceBar component export in an error boundary. BEFORE: The component directly calls useOthers() and renders avatars. If Liveblocks connection fails, an unhandled error propagates and crashes the host page. AFTER: Create a small PresenceBarErrorBoundary class component (or use a try-catch wrapper component) within the same file. When an error is caught, render a minimal fallback: a small muted text 'Presence unavailable' with a RefreshCw icon button that calls window.location.reload(). Export the wrapped version as the default. Also add a try-catch around the useOthers() call result processing so that malformed presence data does not throw. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: EntityPresence overflow button missing aria-expanded and popup pattern
issues.description: The '+N more' overflow button in PresenceDots has an aria-label but no aria-expanded, no popup/tooltip behavior, and no keyboard handler. Users cannot discover who the additional viewers are without a mouse hover that does not exist.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx, find the overflow button (the one showing '+N' for additional viewers). BEFORE: It is a button with aria-label but no onClick handler, no aria-expanded attribute, and no tooltip or popup to list the hidden viewers. Keyboard and screen reader users cannot access the full viewer list. AFTER: Add a useState toggle (overflowOpen). On click or Enter/Space keydown, toggle overflowOpen. When open, render a small absolutely-positioned dropdown div below the button listing all overflow viewer names. Add aria-expanded={overflowOpen} and aria-haspopup='true' to the button. Add role='listbox' or role='list' to the dropdown with each name as a role='listitem'. Add an onBlur handler that closes the dropdown when focus leaves the button+dropdown group. Ensure the dropdown has background: colors.surfaceRaised, border: '1px solid ' + colors.border, borderRadius: borderRadius.md, padding: spacing.sm, boxShadow: shadows.md, and zIndex: 50. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore has basic presence via its Quick Capture collaboration but no real-time edit conflict guards or avatar presence dots on individual entities. SiteSync's EditConflictGuard and PresenceBar are genuine differentiators, but the lack of robust error handling and accessibility gaps mean they feel fragile in demos. Fieldwire has no multi-user presence at all. Autodesk BIM Collaborate has strong co-authoring presence for 3D models but nothing comparable for RFIs or punch lists.
summary: The Collaboration & Team module has a solid architectural foundation with Liveblocks presence integration, optimistic locking via EditConflictGuard, and real-time entity presence dots. However, it scores low because the components lack essential accessibility attributes (no keyboard focus rings, missing aria-expanded on overflow buttons, invisible placeholder divs causing phantom layout gaps), have no error boundaries around Liveblocks calls (connection failures crash the host page), and the conflict banner is not responsive below 480px. The real-time score is the highest dimension at 55 thanks to the working presence store and subscription model, but reliability is undermined by missing error handling. Fixing the five identified issues will improve accessibility by approximately 15 points, mobile-first by 10, and error handling by 8, lifting the module from 38 to approximately 48 overall.
